source: BEP Research — public pricing pages, earnings calls (Q3 FY2026 / Q1 2026 calendar), SEC EDGAR XBRL (auto-refreshed daily), press releases. See data/static/platform-disclosures.json for the latest machine-verified quarterly figures.
methodology: Token Cost Waterfall maps the margin stack from GPU silicon to end-customer. The platform 'token margin' is (customer price per interaction − blended model cost per interaction) / customer price. modelCost is DERIVED LIVE from data/token-pricing/current.json by weighting each platform's modelMix against today's market prices, blended across input and output using a 70/30 input/output default (overridable per platform via inputOutputSplit when workload skew is known). Proprietary in-house models use a stated marginal-cost estimate. Confidence levels: HIGH = public pricing page (pricing) or earnings disclosure (cost), MEDIUM = earnings call disclosure + estimate, MEDIUM-LOW = bottom-up derivation with stated assumptions, LOW = analyst inference. The chart's 'PRICING VERIFIED' badge means the customer-side price is public — it does NOT mean the token-margin computation is verified, because no platform publicly discloses tokens-per-interaction. Token-margin EXCLUDES hosting, R&D, sales, support, performance guarantees, and forward-deployed engineering — it isolates the input-cost variable that compounds with LLMflation.

tokenTierFramework.summary: Each platform's tokensPerInteraction is anchored to one of six sourced tiers, so the assumption can be defended rather than waved at. Anthropic's published baseline: 'agents typically use about 4× more tokens than chat interactions, and multi-agent systems use about 15× more tokens than chats.' This calibrates the tier ranges below.
tokenTierFramework.anthropicMultiplier: Anthropic, How we built our multi-agent research system (engineering blog, 2025): 4x for single-agent tool-using vs chat; 15x for multi-agent.

tier1-chat.label: Chat / Q&A (no RAG)
tier1-chat.source: Anthropic chat baseline; Notion historical 2,048 token context

tier2-rag.label: RAG over documents
tier2-rag.source: NVIDIA Developer Blog reranker eval; AI21 query-dependent chunking: 5-20 chunks, 2K-10K tokens

tier3-code-completion.label: Code completion (per completion)
tier3-code-completion.source: Simform Engineering: 'small inline + short comment ~5 tokens returns ~20 tokens of code'; Copilot Chat context 128-192K; heavy users 50K-200K/day

tier4-agentic-resolution.label: Agentic tool-calling loop (per resolution)
tier4-agentic-resolution.source: Anthropic 15x chat multiplier; Salesforce publicly bills 10,000 tokens/action with multi-action conversations; KAMI benchmark ~32K tokens/item

tier5-coding-agent.label: Multi-turn coding agent (per task)
tier5-coding-agent.source: AgencyBench (arXiv 2601.11044): 138 tasks average 1M tokens and 90 tool calls. Anthropic: 'agent completing a code review task used 120,000 tokens (~$1.80)'

tier6-doc-analysis.label: Enterprise heavy-doc analysis
tier6-doc-analysis.source: arXiv 2601.14470 Tokenomics: input tokens 53.9% of consumption; Code Review phase 59.4% of total tokens

tokenMarginCaveat: Platform 'token margin' is gross margin on the token-cost line only. It is NOT company-level gross margin. Hosting, fine-tuning, customer support, sales commissions, performance guarantees, forward-deployed engineers, and agent R&D sit on top of token cost and reduce true gross margin. Verified-pricing × modeled-tokens × token-only-COGS does not equal verified-margin. Read each number as: 'of every dollar of platform revenue, X cents goes to model providers' — not 'X% gross margin.'
proprietaryCostMethodology: Each proprietaryCostPerM is calibrated to one of three anchors: (a) the vendor's own published API rate where available — Cohere Command R+ ($2.50/M) maps to cohere.com/pricing; Grok-4 ($3.50/M) and Grok-Code ($2.80/M) map to xAI's API pricing; Snowflake Arctic ($1.20/M) and Databricks DBRX ($1.50/M) map to credit-converted hosting rates; (b) the small-model API equivalent for fine-tuned proprietary models with no public price — xGen, Now LLM, Fin Apex 1.0, SAP-LLM, Oracle-LLM, Rovo Search land in the $0.30-$0.80/M band, calibrated against gpt-5-mini ($0.51/M output) and Haiku-class ($1-2/M output) market rates with a discount for internal serving; (c) GPU marginal cost for first-party models on captive infra. These are estimates, not disclosures — sensitivity to ±50% on any single proprietary rate is small at the platform level because the model mix is diversified.

platforms.name: Agentforce
platforms.modelProviderNote: Multi-model routing. xGen for narrow dev tasks (proprietary), GPT-5 default, Claude Sonnet 4.6 via Amazon Bedrock for complex reasoning. BYOLLM supported via Einstein Trust Layer.

customerPricing.altPricing: Flex Credits at $0.10/action (May 2025) is the recommended deployment for most new enterprises; per-user licenses also available at $125-650/user/month. The $2/conversation SKU is the original pricing and still listed but no longer dominant.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Salesforce publicly bills 10,000 tokens per action (salesforce.com/agentforce/pricing). A typical conversation spans 3-5 actions = 30-50K tokens, so we hold at 40K. Cross-check: 3.2T tokens disclosed Q3 FY26 ÷ implied paid interactions ≈ 32-64K tokens per conversation. Agentforce is RAG-heavy (CRM record context + tool-call traces + multi-turn reasoning) — 80/20 input/output. The 10K we had before was a chat-loop assumption; agentic systems run 4-15x heavier per Anthropic's multi-agent research blog.

margins.notes: Q3 FY26 (Dec 3, 2025): Agentforce + Data 360 combined ARR ~$1.4B, +114% YoY. Agentforce-only ARR surpassed $540M, +330%. 3.2 trillion tokens processed. 18,500+ Agentforce deals closed since launch — only 9,500+ are paid, so roughly half remain on free/trial (relevant context for the headline 'paid deals' figure). Pricing model has fragmented: $2/conversation is the original SKU but Flex Credits ($0.10/action, May 2025) is the recommended deployment for most enterprises, and per-user licenses at $125-650/user/month are now also available. Token-margin computed against the $2/conv SKU should be read as the legacy-SKU ceiling, not a blended ASP.

platforms.name: AI Agents
platforms.modelProviderNote: Multi-LLM failover for latency. Routes simple intents to cheaper models.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Tier-4 agentic resolution. Per-resolution range is wide (10K-250K) — Zendesk's number is held at 4K because (a) Zendesk explicitly bills only on full resolution with no human handoff, biasing toward simpler tickets that resolve, (b) heavy routing to fast-path models (Haiku, Flash) compresses the per-resolution token budget. Push the sensitivity slider to 3x (12K) to model resolutions that involve more turns / longer context retrieval.

margins.notes: Private (Hellman & Friedman, $10.2B take-private 2022). Dynamic pricing plan released 2025 allows remixing seats vs resolutions.

platforms.dataGap: Private company — no revenue disclosure post-2022 privatization.

platforms.name: Fin AI Agent
platforms.modelProviderNote: Switched from OpenAI to Anthropic Claude for Fin 2 (Oct 2024). Fin Apex 1.0 (proprietary post-trained) beats GPT-5.4 and Claude Sonnet 4.6 on CS benchmarks (73.1% resolution rate). Apex included at no extra cost.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Tier-4 agentic resolution. The aggregate is low (5K) because Fin Apex 1.0 (proprietary, post-trained) handles ~50% of resolutions at near-zero marginal cost; Claude Sonnet/Haiku absorb the complex tail. Effective blended cost is dominated by the non-Apex tail. The friend-review stress-test at 6x (30K) still leaves >94% token margin — i.e., Fin's economics are robust to this assumption.

margins.notes: Proprietary Apex 1.0 is the margin-expansion play. Anthropic itself is a Fin customer. Private. Caveats not in token-margin math: (1) Fin resolves ~67% of issues — Intercom pays inference tokens on every attempt but only collects on successful resolutions, so ~33% of inference is unbilled overhead; (2) Intercom backs Fin with a performance guarantee up to $1M if resolution targets aren't met, which is a real COGS liability; (3) Fin uses forward-deployed engineers as a customer-success/learning engine — material headcount that hits company-level gross margin even when token-margin holds high.

platforms.dataGap: Private company — no revenue or margin disclosure.

platforms.name: Now Assist
platforms.modelProviderNote: Claude as default for Build Agent (Jan 2026 Anthropic partnership). Open ecosystem — customers can choose LLMs.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Tier-2 RAG. Now Assist queries pull ITSM record context (incidents, CMDB, knowledge articles) + multi-turn reasoning. 6K is conservative for a typical workflow; complex 'Build Agent' tasks land in Tier-4 territory (10K-50K). 10 interactions/day reflects a fulfiller persona — not an end-user CSAT load.

margins.notes: Now Assist ACV >$600M (Q4 FY2025), tracking $1B by year-end 2026. 31% non-GAAP op margin.

platforms.dataGap: Per-user pricing not publicly listed. ACV disclosed but per-seat breakdown estimated.

platforms.name: M365 Copilot
platforms.modelProviderNote: Microsoft owns 49% of OpenAI. Exclusive cloud provider. Captive supply at sub-API cost.

customerPricing.altPricing: Business/SMB: $21/user/month. Copilot Pro: $20/month. M365 E3 rising to $39/user/month July 2026.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Tier-2 RAG over user docs (Word, Excel, Outlook, Teams transcripts). 4K per interaction sits at the low end of the RAG band — Microsoft has been aggressively tuning retrieval to bound context. 12 interactions/day for an active Copilot seat (Microsoft has not disclosed; estimate). 50% internal discount applied because Microsoft owns 49% of OpenAI and is the exclusive cloud provider — effective cost is closer to GPU economics than API list.

margins.notes: Microsoft effectively pays itself for inference via OpenAI. M365 base price increase ($36→$39, July 2026) absorbs AI cost.

platforms.name: GitHub Copilot
platforms.modelProviderNote: Multi-model. Power users hit premium-request caps to limit token exposure.

customerPricing.altPricing: Pro+ at $39/user/month. Enterprise at $39+. Free tier with 2K completions/month.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Tier-3 code completion. Per-completion in the 0.5K-8K band (Simform Engineering: small inline + short comment ~5 tokens returns ~20 tokens). We hold 6K average to reflect that 'agent mode' completions (which Anthropic frames as 4x chat) increasingly dominate vs simple autocomplete. 25/day is a heavy-user estimate; light users (~8/day) are clearly profitable, heavy users (50+/day, often power devs) trigger the premium-request caps Microsoft introduced 2026. Real per-user variance is 10x.

margins.notes: GitHub disclosed Copilot 'approaching profitability' in aggregate (late 2025). Request caps introduced 2026 to control cost.

platforms.dataGap: Actual per-user token consumption varies 10x between light and heavy users.

platforms.name: Joule AI
platforms.modelProviderNote: AI Unit pricing intentionally opaque — obscures token economics.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: AI Unit → token mapping not disclosed. Opaque pricing unit prevents reliable margin computation.

margins.notes: SAP Q1 2026 (reported Apr 23, 2026): Cloud revenue EUR 5.96B, +27% YoY at constant currencies. Cloud ERP Suite +30%. Current cloud backlog EUR 21.9B, +25%. Cloud gross margin ~73%. Management flagged Q2 deceleration from one-time quarter-specific tailwinds.

platforms.dataGap: AI Unit to token conversion unknown. Cannot compute per-token margin.

platforms.name: Workspace + Gemini
platforms.modelProviderNote: Vertically integrated TPU → Gemini → Workspace. Lowest cost producer.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Tier-1 chat / light RAG (Gemini in Docs/Gmail/Sheets). 3K per interaction reflects short chat-style usage. 60% TPU-internal discount: Google operates Gemini on captive TPU clusters at substantially lower marginal cost than the API list price.

margins.notes: Folding AI into base subscription is penetration strategy — monetizes through retention.

platforms.name: Fusion Applications AI
platforms.modelProviderNote: Cohere primary via strategic partnership. Oracle proprietary models for specific domains.

customerPricing.altPricing: Bundled into Fusion Cloud subscriptions; AI premium ~20% uplift.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Tier-2 RAG over Fusion ERP records. 5K per interaction at the low end of the RAG band — structured queries are usually shorter than open-ended doc analysis. 6 interactions/day reflects ERP user persona (fewer but heavier queries vs Copilot user). Domain models (Cohere Command R+, Oracle-LLM) handle structured retrievals at lower cost.

margins.notes: Oracle Q3 FY26 (reported Mar 10, 2026): Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) revenue $4.9B, +84% YoY; Cloud Applications $4.0B, +13%. Total revenue $17.2B, +22%. RPO ended at $553B (+325% YoY) on AI-infrastructure backlog. OCI margin ~38%.

platforms.dataGap: AI premium not separately disclosed; per-user pricing inferred from partner contracts.

platforms.name: Firefly + GenStudio
platforms.modelProviderNote: Adobe Firefly is image diffusion; LLM portion uses Anthropic. Credits monthly grant in CC.

customerPricing.altPricing: 1000 credits/month included in Creative Cloud All Apps ($60/month).

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Firefly is image-priced per generative credit, not token-priced. Image diffusion dominates cost; the text/caption LLM portion is negligible. Token-margin metric is not the right lens — see Adobe Q1 FY26 DM segment 88% gross margin for the right number.

margins.notes: Firefly trained on licensed Adobe Stock corpus — defensible IP moat. Image-credit pricing means margin is a function of GPU diffusion cost (cents per image), not LLM token cost. Adobe DM segment runs 88% gross margin.

platforms.dataGap: Image generation isn't strictly token-priced. Credits map to GPU-seconds, not tokens.

platforms.name: Breeze
platforms.modelProviderNote: Multi-LLM under Breeze Intelligence umbrella. Free Breeze tier in all paid plans; premium agents charged separately.

customerPricing.altPricing: Breeze Intelligence credits $30/month. Free Breeze Copilot in all paid plans.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Tier-2 RAG. Breeze Copilot pulls CRM record context for typical sales/marketing workflows. 4.5K mid-band for the tier. 8 interactions/day reflects active CRM user.

margins.notes: HubSpot Q1 2026 (reported May 7, 2026): revenue $881M, +23% YoY. Non-GAAP operating margin 18%. AI monetization accelerating — core seat users +90% YoY, credit consumption +67% QoQ. Full-year guide raised to ~$3.70B.

platforms.name: Rovo
platforms.modelProviderNote: Free Rovo Chat in all paid Cloud plans (Feb 2026). Premium agents charged separately.

customerPricing.altPricing: Rovo Chat included free with Cloud plans. Agents add-on at $20/user/month.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Tier-1 chat / lightweight RAG over Jira/Confluence. Rovo Chat is included free in all paid Cloud plans (Feb 2026) — the margin computation here is for the paid Rovo Agents add-on at $20/user/month. Free Chat is a platform-lock investment, not a margin product.

margins.notes: Free Rovo Chat is platform-lock play. Atlassian Q3 FY26 (ended Mar 31, 2026, reported Apr 30, 2026): Cloud revenue $1.13B, +29% YoY; total revenue $1.79B, +32% YoY. 34% non-GAAP operating margin. Cloud gross margin ~85%.

platforms.dataGap: Free Rovo Chat distribution makes per-user margin ambiguous. Premium Agents not yet at scale.

platforms.name: Notion AI
platforms.modelProviderNote: Primary on Anthropic Claude. Built directly on frontier APIs, no proprietary layer.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Tier-2 RAG. Notion uses long page context (full page content), which inflates input tokens — 80/20 input/output skew. 4K aggregate sits at the low end of the RAG band; complex pages with long context skew much higher. 6 interactions/day reflects an active Notion AI user.

margins.notes: Private. Last primary round 2021; secondary tender at $10B (2022) and $11B (Dec 2025). No primary raise in four years — Bloomberg sources point to a potential 2026 IPO. No proprietary model — fully exposed to model-provider pricing.

platforms.dataGap: Private company. Token margin highly sensitive to Anthropic pricing.

platforms.name: ChatGPT Enterprise
platforms.modelProviderNote: First-party — no margin leakage to external model provider. Token cost = internal GPU cost only.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Mostly Tier-2 RAG (file uploads, web search, retrieval) with occasional Tier-5 coding-agent tasks. 8K average reflects enterprise power-user load. 20 interactions/day estimated for active seats. 70% internal discount because OpenAI is first-party — no model-provider markup, only GPU cost.

margins.notes: OpenAI is first-party — pure GPU economics, no model-provider markup.

platforms.dataGap: Enterprise pricing custom-negotiated; $60/user/month is midpoint estimate.

platforms.name: Cortex AI
platforms.modelProvider: Anthropic + Meta + Snowflake Arctic + customer choice
platforms.modelProviderNote: Cortex offers Claude, Llama, Arctic, Mistral. Customer pays Snowflake credits which include compute + model markup.

customerPricing.altPricing: Per-token: $0.0006-$0.024/1K input tokens depending on model.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Snowflake marks up wholesale model API rates via the credit system. Markup of ~2.2x is derived by comparing Cortex per-token credit consumption rates (snowflake.com/legal-files/CreditConsumptionTable.pdf) for, e.g., claude-sonnet-4 (~$0.024/1K input, $0.120/1K output via Cortex) against Anthropic's API list (~$0.003 input / $0.015 output per 1K) — implying ~7-8x output markup pre-volume, settling to ~2-2.5x at typical enterprise mix and prepaid-credit discount. Margin = take rate on top of wholesale model cost passed through, NOT credit price minus token cost. Wholesale flows through to model provider; Snowflake keeps (markup-1)/markup.

margins.notes: Snowflake Q4 FY26 (ended Jan 31, 2026, reported Feb 25, 2026): product revenue $1.23B, +30% YoY. Full-year FY26 product revenue $4.42B+. NRR 125%. Cortex AI fastest-growing component. Headline 99% margin (early data) was wrong — Snowflake is a middleman taking a markup, not pocketing the full credit. True take rate ~55%.

platforms.name: Mosaic AI
platforms.modelProvider: Anthropic + Meta + Mosaic DBRX + customer choice
platforms.modelProviderNote: Foundation Model APIs include DBRX, Llama, Claude, Mixtral. Mosaic ML acquired 2023 ($1.3B).

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Mosaic markup ~2x wholesale model API rate via DBU consumption. Derived by comparing Databricks' Foundation Model API pricing (databricks.com/product/pricing) — e.g., Claude Sonnet at ~$0.0072 input / $0.036 output per 1K DBU-equivalent — against Anthropic API list of ~$0.003 / $0.015 per 1K. Output markup ~2.4x at quoted rates; volume DBU commits compress to ~1.8-2.0x effective. DBRX self-hosted at near-marginal cost in the cheaper DBU tiers. Margin = take rate, not (DBU price − wholesale cost).

margins.notes: Databricks private ($62B valuation). $3B+ ARR, ~35% YoY. Mosaic AI margin ~50% from middleman take rate on model API + DBU compute.

platforms.dataGap: Private. DBU-to-token conversion varies by workload type.

platforms.name: AIP
platforms.modelProviderNote: AIP is orchestration layer over multiple LLMs. Multi-million-dollar enterprise contracts, not per-token.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Token consumption not the binding cost — Palantir margin dominated by Forward Deployed Engineers.

margins.notes: Palantir Q1 2026 (reported May 5, 2026): total revenue $1.633B, +85% YoY (fastest since IPO). US commercial revenue $595M, +133% YoY. Full-year 2026 guidance raised to 71% growth; US commercial guide raised to >$3.224B (+120%+). AIP driving the acceleration.

platforms.dataGap: AIP value proposition is not token-priced — engagement model dominates. Token margin N/A.

platforms.name: Cursor
platforms.modelProviderNote: Cursor Pro at $20/user/month. Heavy Claude routing for code edits.

customerPricing.altPricing: Free tier with 2K completions/month. Business at $40/user/month.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Currently modeled as Tier-3 (completions) — but Cursor's pricing is set against agent-mode usage that lands in Tier-5 (50K-1M tokens/task per AgencyBench). At baseline (6K × 15 = 90K/day = ~2M/month) Cursor is marginal; at 3x sensitivity the slider pushes it deeply negative, which matches the well-known public claim that Anysphere subsidizes power users. The thesis is convert-to-Business-tier, not per-seat margin.

margins.notes: Anysphere private ($9.9B valuation, May 2026). $300M+ ARR reported. Sensitivity-test: at 3x baseline tokens, margin goes deeply negative (matches public claims that power users are loss-making).

platforms.dataGap: Private. Token consumption per power user could be 2-3x stated assumption.

platforms.name: Grok for Business
platforms.modelProviderNote: First-party. xAI operates own GPU clusters (Colossus).

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Tier-2 estimate (no public enterprise-mix disclosure from xAI). 6K × 15/day reflects an active business user. xAI operates Colossus internally, so the cost-per-token is GPU economics — not API list pricing. Enterprise SKU details not formally published.

margins.notes: xAI valuation ~$200B (May 2026). Capex-heavy: Colossus 2 supercomputer cited as $5B+ buildout.

platforms.dataGap: Private. Enterprise pricing not formally published.

platforms.name: Box AI
platforms.modelProvider: Anthropic + OpenAI + AWS Bedrock + Box Hubs
platforms.modelProviderNote: Multi-LLM agentic platform. Box AI Studio launched 2024.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Tier-6 enterprise document analysis. Box AI workloads are doc-heavy (contracts, slide decks, PDFs); 5K is conservative for that tier (typical 20K-60K). 4 interactions/day reflects deliberate per-document review use, not chat-heavy use. 80/20 input/output skew because input context dominates document Q&A.

margins.notes: Box Q4 FY26 (reported Mar 3, 2026): revenue $306M, +9% YoY; full-year FY26 revenue $1.18B, +8%. 28% full-year operating margin. AI-driven suites cited as the growth-acceleration driver vs prior 5% YoY base rate.

platforms.dataGap: AI portion of Enterprise Plus pricing inferred from tier delta.

historicalCostBenchmark_DEPRECATED: The quarterly LLMflation back-test is now derived at build time from data/token-pricing/history.json via quarterlyLLMflationSeries() in src/lib/calculations.ts. This produces a real, era-aware history instead of synthesized constants.

waterfall_summary.gpu_to_token: GPU TCO $/hr ÷ tokens/sec ÷ 3600 × 1M = production cost per million tokens. H100 TCO ~$3.00/hr ÷ 95 tok/s/GPU = ~$8.77/M (Llama 70B GPU-only baseline).
waterfall_summary.token_to_api: Frontier labs sell at 1.5-3x production cost. Anthropic Sonnet 4.6 at $15/M output. Commodity floor ($0.18/M for gpt-oss-120b) is sold at a loss to capture marketshare.
waterfall_summary.api_to_platform: Enterprise platforms charge 20-100x model API cost per interaction. Per-resolution platforms (Zendesk, Intercom) and per-conversation platforms (Salesforce) compound margin fastest because their unit economics are decoupled from token deflation.
compression_points: Per-resolution platforms (Zendesk, Intercom, Salesforce) are insulated from token deflation — they sell outcomes, not tokens. Margin EXPANDS as input cost falls.
Per-user platforms (Microsoft, ServiceNow, HubSpot, Notion) face power-user cost risk. Token consumption scales with user behavior, not contract value.
Proprietary model development (Intercom Apex, Salesforce xGen, Adobe Firefly, Snowflake Arctic, Databricks DBRX) is the durable margin-expansion play.
Consumption-credit platforms (Snowflake, Databricks) sit in between — capture compute markup but expose customers to token volatility.
Free-AI platforms (Atlassian Rovo Chat, Google Workspace) sacrifice direct AI margin to defend platform lock-in.
arbitrage_opportunities: Salesforce charges $2/conversation against ~$0.06 token cost — 33x markup. Any focused vertical agent (legal, healthcare) can undercut massively.
GitHub Copilot at $19/user/month is loss-making for power users. Cap introduction signals Microsoft is finding the profitability boundary.
Cursor at $20/user/month with 30 completions/day is likely token-negative. Anysphere's $9.9B valuation requires margin expansion via proprietary models or pricing increases.
ServiceNow moving to consumption-based pricing — signals confidence that per-token margins are positive.
Google folding Gemini into base Workspace at $4/user implied AI revenue — accepting lower per-AI margin to block competitors.
Notion AI fully exposed to Anthropic pricing (no proprietary model). Margin compresses if Claude rates rise.
Snowflake and Databricks both running ~2-3x model API markup via credit system — durable middleman position as long as data gravity holds.
waterfall_summary.key_finding: Margin concentrates at the distribution layer, not the production layer. Per-resolution platforms (Zendesk, Intercom, Salesforce) capture the highest token-margins because they sell outcomes, not tokens. Per-user platforms face power-user cost risk. The investable insight: the enterprise platform layer earns a 20-100x markup on model-provider cost on the token line — but that 'margin' is gross-of-everything-else (hosting, R&D, sales). The structural advantage is durability: per-resolution pricing decouples revenue from input cost, so as LLMflation continues, margins compound. Proprietary models are the second moat.
